heading: Selection of polling facilities
label: 20102 Selection of polling facilities
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 1973ee–1 of Title 42
Effective DateSection applicable with respect to elections taking place after , see , set out as a note under .Dec. 31, 1985section 9 of Pub. L. 98–435section 20101 of this title

children.heading: Accessibility to all polling places as responsibility of each political subdivision
children.label: a Accessibility to all polling places as responsibility of each political subdivision
children.text: Within each State, except as provided in subsection (b), each political subdivision responsible for conducting elections shall assure that all polling places for Federal elections are accessible to handicapped and elderly voters.
in the case of an emergency, as determined by the chief election officer of the State; or

children.label: A
children.text: determines that all potential polling places have been surveyed and no such accessible place is available, nor is the political subdivision able to make one temporarily accessible, in the area involved; and

children.label: i
children.text: will be assigned to an accessible polling place, or

children.label: ii
children.text: will be provided with an alternative means for casting a ballot on the day of the election.

children.heading: Report to Federal Election Commission
children.label: c Report to Federal Election Commission

children.text: Not later than December 31 of each even-numbered year, the chief election officer of each State shall report to the Federal Election Commission, in a manner to be determined by the Commission, the number of accessible and inaccessible polling places in such State on the date of the preceding general Federal election, and the reasons for any instance of inaccessibility.
Not later than April 30 of each odd-numbered year, the Federal Election Commission shall compile the information reported under paragraph (1) and shall transmit that information to the Congress.
The provisions of this subsection shall only be effective for a period of 10 years beginning on .September 28, 1984
